steps.title: Use the diameter endpoints
steps.content: The diameter endpoints are given directly, so find the diameter length first and then take half of it to get the radius.

steps.title: Calculate the diameter length
steps.content: The diameter endpoints are $(3, 5)$ and $(3, 17)$. Both points have the same $x$-coordinate, so this is a vertical segment. The length is: $$17 - 5 = 12$$

steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content: The radius is half the diameter: $$r = \frac{12}{2} = 6$$
